free_text: Cross sections of the reactions 58Fe(p,g)59Co,
58Fe(p,n)58Co, 55Mn(a,n)58Co, 55Mn(a,p)58Fe and
57Fe(p,n)57Co
5U Pelletron accelerator
Data were received from the author: S.G.Tims
and from table 2.
SD
An Fe target was prepared by evaporation of metallic Fe
enriched to 93.23% in 58Fe and 6.56% in 57Fe, from a
tungsten boat on to a 0.13 mm etched gold backing.
The adopted thicknesses were (5.38+-0.35)x10**18 58Fe
atoms/cm2, (3.79+-0.75)x10**17 57Fe atoms/cm2
A 125cm3 Ge(Li) detector placed 3 cm
from the target in the 55 degr. direction.
Error in the cross section scale, the main
contributors being detector efficiency (6%), fraction
of total yield observed (4%), target thickness (6%),
beam current integration (6%)
Statistical error
An Fe target was prepared by evaporation of metallic Fe
enriched to 93.23% in 58Fe and 6.56% in 57Fe, from a
tungsten boat on to a 0.13 mm etched gold backing.
The adopted thicknesses were (5.38+-0.35)x10**18 58Fe
atoms/cm2, (3.79+-0.75)x10**17 57Fe atoms/cm2
The cross section was measured by observation
of the 70.8 d 58Co activity and also by
observation of the neutron yield.
A 120cm3 Ge(Li) detector placed 14 mm
from the target in the 90 degr. direction.
A second 70cm3 Ge(Li) detector was located 30 cm
from the target in the 55 degr. direction, to measure
the yield of 432 keV gamma-ray.
For neutron counting measurements the target
chamber was a stainless steel tubular cross located
at the center on the neutron detector which consisted
of 32x32x44 cm3 paraffin castle embedded in which
were six 3He-filled proportinal counters.
Error in the cross section scale, the main
contributors being gamma-ray detector efficiency - 8%,
target thickness (6%), beam  current integration (6%).
Error in the cross section scale, the main
contributors being neutron detector efficiency - 12%,
target thickness (6%), beam current integration (6%).
Statistical error
